title: Rust language overview
text: Rust is a systems programming language focused on memory safety without a garbage collector. Ownership, borrowing, and lifetimes enforce safety at compile time. Cargo is the official package manager and build tool. The Rust toolchain is distributed via rustup.

title: Rust async runtime
text: Asynchronous code in Rust uses the async and await keywords plus a runtime executor such as Tokio or async-std. Futures are lazy and require a runtime to make progress. Tokio is the most widely deployed runtime for networked services in Rust.

title: Python language overview
text: Python is a dynamically typed, interpreted general-purpose programming language. It emphasizes readability and uses indentation to define blocks. CPython is the reference implementation. Pip and virtualenv are standard tools for dependency management.

title: Python type hints
text: Type hints in Python were introduced in PEP 484. They are optional annotations checked statically by tools like mypy. Type hints do not affect runtime behaviour but improve IDE assistance and document intent.

title: Go language overview
text: Go is a statically typed, compiled language from Google. It features lightweight goroutines for concurrency, channels for communication, and a garbage collector. The Go toolchain includes formatter, race detector, and module system.

title: JavaScript ECMAScript
text: JavaScript is the implementation of the ECMAScript standard managed by TC39. New language features are proposed through the TC39 stages process. Modern engines like V8, SpiderMonkey, and JavaScriptCore implement the spec.

title: Linux kernel
text: The Linux kernel is a free monolithic Unix-like kernel originally written by Linus Torvalds. It powers servers, desktops, smartphones, and embedded devices. The kernel exposes system calls and uses cgroups and namespaces for isolation.

title: Linux containers
text: Linux containers use kernel features such as namespaces and cgroups to isolate processes. Docker popularized container packaging. Container runtimes like containerd and CRI-O implement the OCI runtime specification.

title: macOS Darwin kernel
text: macOS is built on the Darwin operating system, which combines the XNU kernel with userland tools from BSD and NeXTSTEP. It includes the launchd init system and the Mach microkernel as part of the XNU hybrid design.

title: Windows NT kernel
text: Windows NT is a hybrid kernel originally designed by Dave Cutler. It supports preemptive multitasking, multiprocessing, and user-mode subsystems. Modern Windows versions descend from the NT codebase.

title: TCP transport protocol
text: The Transmission Control Protocol provides reliable, ordered byte stream delivery over IP networks. It uses sequence numbers, acknowledgments, and a three-way handshake to establish connections. Congestion control is governed by algorithms such as Reno and CUBIC.

title: UDP transport protocol
text: The User Datagram Protocol is a connectionless transport that delivers individual messages without ordering or retransmission guarantees. It is used by DNS, NTP, and many real-time multimedia protocols. UDP has lower overhead than TCP.

title: HTTP request-response
text: HTTP is a stateless application protocol structured as request and response messages. HTTP/1.1 multiplexes via persistent connections; HTTP/2 multiplexes streams over a single TCP connection; HTTP/3 runs over QUIC on UDP.

title: DNS name resolution
text: The Domain Name System resolves human-readable domain names to IP addresses through a hierarchical, distributed database. Resolvers query authoritative nameservers, often using UDP on port 53 with TCP fallback for large responses.

title: Big-O notation
text: Big-O notation describes the asymptotic upper bound on a function's growth. In computer science it characterizes how the running time or memory of an algorithm scales with input size. Common bounds include constant, logarithmic, linear, quadratic, and exponential.

title: Turing machine
text: A Turing machine is an abstract computational model that reads and writes symbols on an infinite tape according to a finite set of rules. The model captures what is computable in principle. Alan Turing introduced it in 1936.

title: Lambda calculus
text: Lambda calculus is a formal system for expressing computation through function abstraction and application. Introduced by Alonzo Church in the 1930s, it underlies functional programming and is Turing-complete.

title: Sorting algorithm complexity
text: Comparison-based sorting algorithms have a lower bound of n log n on the number of comparisons required. Merge sort and heapsort achieve this bound in the worst case. Quicksort is n log n on average but n squared in the worst case.

title: Graph traversal
text: Depth-first search and breadth-first search are the two foundational graph traversal algorithms. DFS uses a stack and explores deep paths first; BFS uses a queue and explores level by level. Both run in linear time relative to nodes plus edges.

title: Public key cryptography
text: Public key cryptography uses a pair of keys: a public key for encryption or signature verification, and a private key for decryption or signing. RSA and elliptic-curve schemes are widely deployed. Diffie-Hellman enables secure key exchange over insecure channels.
